data.title: CoreWeave plans $1.25B senior notes offering; shares up ~7%
data.description:
• CoreWeave (CRWV) intends to offer $1,250M aggregate principal amount of senior notes due 2031 in a private offering, it said Thursay. The notes will be guaranteed on a senior unsecured basis by certain wholly-owned subsidiaries of CoreWeave.
  • CoreWeave intends to use the proceeds from the offering for general corporate purposes, including, without limitation, repayment of outstanding indebtedness, and to pay fees, costs and expenses in connection with the offering of the notes. The company's shares rose about 7% in early trading. 
  • Separately, CoreWeave (CRWV) inked an expanded, long-term agreement with Meta Platforms (META) to provide AI cloud capacity through December 2032 for approximately $21 billion. With this deal, the two companies are continuing their existing relationship, increasing support for Meta’s development and deployment of AI.

data.title: CoreWeave, Meta enter into a $21B expanded AI infrastructure pact
data.description:
CoreWeave (CRWV) shares jumped 7% premarket on Thursday after it announced an expanded, long-term pact with Meta Platforms (META) to provide AI cloud capacity through December 2032 for ~$21B.

With this deal, the two companies are continuing their existing relationship, increasing support for Meta’s development and deployment of AI. Meta (META) will leverage CoreWeave’s (CRWV) AI cloud platform to scale inference workloads.

“This is another example that leading companies are choosing CoreWeave’s AI cloud to run their most demanding workloads,” said Michael Intrator, co-founder, CEO, chairman of CoreWeave.

CRWV +7.5% premarket to $95.61

Source: Press Release

data.title: Amazon's chip unit's annual revenue run rate over $20B, discusses capex returns
data.description:
Amazon's (AMZN) CEO Andy Jassy discussed the company's chip business and returns on the company's massive $200B capital expenditure plans for 2026, and other things in his 2025 letter to shareholders.

"We’re not investing approximately $200 billion in capex in 2026 on a hunch. The recent OpenAI (OPENAI) commitment (over $100 billion) is an example of this, but there are several other customer agreements completed (and unannounced), or deep in process. Of the AWS capex we expect to spend in 2026, much of which will be monetized in 2027-2028, we already have customer commitments for a substantial portion of it," said Jassy in the letter, which was out on Thursday.

During the company's fourth-quarter earnings call in February, Jassy said that the company expects to invest about $200B in capital expenditures, or capex, across Amazon, but predominantly in Amazon Web Services, or AWS. He added that the company has very high demand and customers want AWS for core and AI workloads, and "we're monetizing capacity as fast as we can install it."

Jassy said in the letter that the annual revenue run rate for the company's chips business (inclusive of Graviton, Trainium, and Nitro) is now over $20B and growing triple-digit percentages year-over-year.

"To dimensionalize this versus other chips companies, that run rate is somewhat understated by our currently only monetizing our chips through EC2. If our chips business was a stand-alone business, and sold chips produced this year to AWS and other third parties (as other leading chips companies do), our annual run rate would be ~$50 billion. There’s so much demand for our chips that it’s quite possible we’ll sell racks of them to third parties in the future," Jassy noted.

Jassy added that at scale, Amazon expects the Trainium AI chips will save it tens of billions of capex dollars per year and provide several hundred basis points of operating margin advantage versus relying on others' chips for inference.

AI inference is the process of running a trained AI model to produce predictions or conclusions on new, unseen data.

Jassy noted that Trainium3, which started shipping at the start of 2026 and is 30% to 40% more price-performant than Trainium2, is nearly fully subscribed.

"A significant chunk of Trainium4, which is still about 18 months from broad availability, has already been reserved. And, Amazon Bedrock, AWS’s primary (and very fast-growing) inference service, runs most of its inference on Trainium. Demand for Trainium is booming," Jassy noted.

Jassy spoke about the company's partnership with Nvidia (NVDA) but also about competition.

"We have a strong partnership with NVIDIA, will always have customers who choose to run NVIDIA, and we will continue to make AWS the best place to run NVIDIA. However, customers want better price-performance," Jassy noted.

Jassy said that the second version of its custom AI silicon, Trainium2, had about 30% better price-performance than comparable graphics processing units, or GPUs, and has largely sold out.

In addition, Jassy said that AWS added 3.9 gigawatts, or GW, of new power capacity in 2025, expects to double total power capacity by the end of 2027, and is monetizing that capacity as fast as it is installed. He added that in the fourth quarter of 2025, AWS reported 24% year-over-year growth with a $142B revenue run rate.

"That’s a lot of absolute growth. And yet, we still have capacity constraints that yield unserved demand. [As an aside, two large AWS customers have already asked if they could buy *all* of our Graviton instance capacity in 2026 (Graviton is our widely-adopted custom CPU chip)—we can’t agree to these requests given other customers’ needs, but it gives you an idea of the demand.]," said Jassy.

Shares of Amazon were largely flat but in the green premarket on Thursday.

data.title: OpenAI pauses Stargate UK data center citing energy costs, regulatory hurdles
data.description:
OpenAI (OPENAI) is pausing its Stargate artificial intelligence infrastructure project in the UK as it reins in spending ahead of a potential public listing.

Stargate is OpenAI’s global initiative to expand data center capacity for training and running AI models, with projects already underway in the U.S., Norway, and the United Arab Emirates. The UK project had been announced last year in partnership with Nvidia (NVDA) and British data center developer Nscale.

In a statement to Seeking Alpha, an OpenAI spokesperson said, "We see huge potential for the UK's AI future. London is home to our largest international research hub, and we support the Government's ambition to be an AI leader. AI compute is foundational to that goal - we continue to explore Stargate UK and will move forward when the right conditions such as regulation and the cost of energy enable long-term infrastructure investment.”

“In the meantime, we are investing in talent and expanding our local presence, while also delivering on the commitments under our MOU with the Government to adopt frontier AI in UK public services."

OpenAI’s pullback comes as it sharpens focus on core operations while facing rising competition from Anthropic (ANTHRO) and Google (GOOG) (GOOGL). In March, the AI firm shuttered its video-generation app Sora. It also recently scrapped plans to expand its flagship data center campus in Texas with Oracle (ORCL).

The decision is also a setback for the UK government’s ambitions to position the country as a global AI hub, with high-energy costs seen as a key challenge for large-scale data center investments.

data.description:
• Simply Good Foods press release (SMPL): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.45 beats by $0.05.
  • Revenue of $326M (-9.4% Y/Y) misses by $17.87M.

  • Updating Fiscal Year 2026(4) Outlook:

    • Net sales expected to range between $1.31 and $1.35 billion vs $1.44B consensus, or -10% to -7% year-over-year

  • Shares +1.3% PM.

data.title: MacroGenics gains removal of FDA partial hold on lead program
data.description:
Macrogenics (MGNX) shares traded higher in the premarket on Thursday after the U.S. Food and Drug Administration ((FDA)) removed a partial clinical hold imposed on a mid-stage trial for its lead candidate, lorigerlimab.

In February, the FDA slapped the Rockville, Maryland-based biotech with a partial hold on its Phase 2 LINNET study designed to evaluate lorigerlimab in patients with platinum-resistant ovarian cancer ((PROC)) or clear cell gynecologic cancer.

While dosing was allowed to continue, the regulator barred the company from adding new patients to the trial following the decision, which came after four patients who received lorigerlimab experienced safety events.

In a statement on Wednesday, Macrogenics (MGNX) stated that, with the partial hold now lifted, it can begin enrolling new patients to LINNET under a revised trial protocol that includes additional safety measures targeting potential blood- and heart-related side effects.

The company is on track to provide a program update from LINNET in mid-2026.

data.title: Glencore, Taiwan refiner book tankers to load Middle East oil after ceasefire: report
data.description:
Commodities trader Glencore (GLCNF) and Taiwan's state refiner CPC have ​chartered a tanker each to load Middle Eastern crude for Asia, while vessels in the Gulf are preparing to exit ‌via the Strait of Hormuz, a day after the ceasefire in the U.S.-Iran war, Reuters reported on Thursday.

The two-week truce hinges on letting ships pass through the strait, a chokepoint for about 20% of global oil and liquefied natural gas shipments brought to a near standstill by the six-week conflict, sharply driving up global energy prices. Asian refiners rely on the Middle East for ​more than half their supply of crude and naphtha, feedstocks for fuel and petrochemical production.

Taiwanese Economy Minister Kung Ming-hsin told reporters ⁠on Thursday that state-owned refiner CPC had booked one tanker in the Gulf to bring some 2 million barrels of oil. "If passage is possible ​within the next two weeks or so, it can come over," he said.

"With these 2 million barrels, given that we use an average of about 150,000 ​barrels per day, this can provide an additional half month or more of usage. So this will help ease ... the situation."

Glencore has provisionally chartered a Suezmax tanker to load from ​Iraq's Basra Oil Terminal at W860 on the Worldscale industry measure used to calculate freight rates, two shipping sources said. The tanker, capable of ​holding 1 million barrels of oil, is already in the Gulf, the report added.

Glencore's earlier attempt to book a very large crude carrier (VLCC) Asian Lion was ‌not successful, ⁠the sources said.

Middle East oil producers such as Iraq are ready to restore crude exports once the Strait reopens. Spot VLCC shipping rates on the route, more commonly known as TD3C , have more than doubled to W513 from W230 on February 27, before the war started, LSEG data showed.
